iterations.theme: WWDC 25: Developer Relations, New UI/UX, iPadOS, and On-Device AI Models
iterations.reason_for_cut: This iteration covers the initial impressions of WWDC, Apple's developer relations, major platform UI/UX changes like Fluid Glass, iPadOS enhancements, and a detailed discussion on the potential and limitations of on-device AI models, concluding before shifting to App Store specific tools.

chapters.topic: Podcast Introduction and Host Introductions
WWDC On-Site vs. Online Experience
Apple's Developer Relations and PR Strategy

Fluid Glass as an Opportunity for Apps
Preparing for Fluid Glass: WWDC Sessions and Human Interface Guidelines
Fluid Glass: React Native vs. Flutter Implications
iPadOS 26: Multi-Windowing and Background Tasks
Speculation: iPadOS Changes and Foldable iPhones
Skepticism and Optimism on On-Device Language Models (LLMs)
API Design for On-Device LLMs and Specialized Models
Practical Application of Image Playgrounds in Dark Noise App

iterations.theme: App Store Enhancements, Attribution, and Future Outlook for Apple Developers
iterations.reason_for_cut: This iteration focuses on updates to App Store Analytics, the App Store Connect API, the Ad Attribution Kit, and concludes with an overall sentiment and future outlook for Apple and its developer ecosystem. It naturally follows the discussion of core platform features.
